title.en: Onkelos, Aquila, and Other Famous Converts Who Shaped Judaism
title.zh: 昂克罗斯、阿奎拉和其他塑造犹太教的著名皈依者
title.he: אונקלוס, אקילס וגרים מפורסמים אחרים שעיצבו את היהדות

cultural_parallels: 入乡随俗 (ru xiang sui su)
礼贤下士 (recognizing talent regardless of origin)
target_audience: Chinese speakers exploring conversion to Orthodox Judaism
key_figures: Onkelos (c. 35-120 CE) - Translator of Torah into Aramaic
Ruth - Moabite convert, ancestor of King David
Shemaya and Avtalyon (c. 1st century BCE) - Heads of Sanhedrin, descendants of converts
Abraham - First convert in Jewish tradition
Obadiah the Proselyte (12th century) - Medieval composer
main_themes: Converts have shaped Jewish thought and practice throughout history
Background does not determine destiny in Judaism
Conversion is a choice that parallels Abraham's journey
Fresh perspectives from converts enrich Jewish tradition
Judaism welcomes sincere seekers regardless of origin
bibliography: The Convert's Journey by Rabbi Lawrence J. Epstein
Maimonides's Letter to Obadiah the Proselyte
Ruth Rabbah - Midrashic commentary on the Book of Ruth
Talmud Bavli, Megillah 13a - On the greatness of converts
From Sinai to China by Xu Xin - On the Kaifeng Jewish community
author: Torah Light Writing Team
